description:
A researcher has two raw CSV files that must be migrated into a single SQLite
        research database, then validated.  You (the assistant) should perform the
        end-to-end migration and produce a human-readable validation log.

        Initial material already present on the file-system
        ---------------------------------------------------
        1. Directory: /home/user/datasets
        2. Raw CSV files inside that directory:
           • /home/user/datasets/species_A.csv
           • /home/user/datasets/species_B.csv

        Target artefacts that **must** exist when you are finished
        -----------------------------------------------------------
        A. SQLite database: /home/user/datasets/research.db  
           – Create two tables named `species_a` and `species_b`, each with the
             following column order and SQLite datatypes  
               (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT, weight REAL, length REAL).

           – Load every row from species_A.csv into table `species_a`
             and every row from species_B.csv into table `species_b`.
             The header line of each CSV must be ignored during the import.

           – Create a third table called `species_all` having exactly the same
             schema.  Insert every record from `species_a` and `species_b`
             into `species_all` (a simple UNION-ALL of both source tables).

        B. Validation log: /home/user/datasets/migration_validation.log  
           – Overwrite the file if it already exists.  
           – The log **must contain exactly seven newline-terminated lines** in the
             precise key = value format shown below (no extra spaces, upper-case
             keys, colon as delimiter, values must be plain integers except the
             final SUCCESS/FAIL string).  The keys **must appear in this order**:

               SPECIES_A_ROWS:<integer>
               SPECIES_B_ROWS:<integer>
               COMBINED_ROWS:<integer>
               MISSING_NAME:<integer>
               MISSING_WEIGHT:<integer>
               MISSING_LENGTH:<integer>
               MIGRATION_STATUS:SUCCESS

             • SPECIES_A_ROWS is the exact number of data rows imported into
               table `species_a`.  
             • SPECIES_B_ROWS is the exact number of data rows imported into
               table `species_b`.  
             • COMBINED_ROWS  is the total row count in table `species_all`.  
             • MISSING_NAME   is the count of records in `species_all`
               whose `name` column is NULL or an empty string ''.  
             • MISSING_WEIGHT is the count of records whose `weight` is NULL.  
             • MISSING_LENGTH is the count of records whose `length` is NULL.  
             • MIGRATION_STATUS must be SUCCESS **only** if the numeric values
               are internally consistent, i.e.  
                 COMBINED_ROWS == SPECIES_A_ROWS + SPECIES_B_ROWS.

        Required sequence (high level)
        ------------------------------
        1. Verify that /home/user/datasets exists and contains the two CSV files.  
        2. Create /home/user/datasets/research.db and the two source tables.  
        3. Import the CSV data into their respective tables.  
        4. Build the `species_all` table and populate it.  
        5. Query the database to compute every statistic listed above.  
        6. Generate /home/user/datasets/migration_validation.log in the exact
           format required, using the numbers obtained from the queries.  
        7. If all went well, write MIGRATION_STATUS:SUCCESS; otherwise write
           MIGRATION_STATUS:FAIL.

        Notes & constraints
        -------------------
        • Use only user-level privileges; do not require sudo.  
        • The agent may use the `sqlite3` CLI, Python, Awk, or any other common
          user-level tool available in a standard Linux environment.  
        • Make sure the log file’s parent directory already exists before
          writing.  
        • Do **not** delete the original CSV files.  
        • Do not print or echo the entire contents of the CSV files to stdout;
          only display concise progress information.

        Passing criteria (automated test)
        ---------------------------------
        The grader will:
        1. Open /home/user/datasets/research.db and verify that three tables
           exist and that their row counts and NULL statistics match the numbers
           written in the validation log.  
        2. Confirm that /home/user/datasets/migration_validation.log is present,
           contains exactly seven lines, that the keys are in the correct order,
           and that MIGRATION_STATUS is SUCCESS.  
        Any deviation will result in failure.
truth:
Pre-existing files placed by the testing harness before the agent starts
        -----------------------------------------------------------------------
        File: /home/user/datasets/species_A.csv
        -----8<----- BEGIN CONTENT
id,name,weight,length
1,Panthera leo,190,2.0
2,Panthera pardus,,1.5
3,,120,1.1
4,Panthera tigris,220,2.3
5,Acinonyx jubatus,72,
-----8<----- END CONTENT

        File: /home/user/datasets/species_B.csv
        -----8<----- BEGIN CONTENT
id,name,weight,length
6,Canis lupus,45,1.3
7,Canis latrans,15,0.9
8,,33,1.0
9,Canis aureus,,1.1
-----8<----- END CONTENT

        Ground-truth statistics the grader will assert
        ----------------------------------------------
        SPECIES_A_ROWS  = 5
        SPECIES_B_ROWS  = 4
        COMBINED_ROWS   = 9
        MISSING_NAME    = 2   (row-ids 3 and 8)
        MISSING_WEIGHT  = 2   (row-ids 2 and 9)
        MISSING_LENGTH  = 1   (row-id 5)

        Expected final log file exact content
        -------------------------------------
        SPECIES_A_ROWS:5
        SPECIES_B_ROWS:4
        COMBINED_ROWS:9
        MISSING_NAME:2
        MISSING_WEIGHT:2
        MISSING_LENGTH:1
        MIGRATION_STATUS:SUCCESS
